transcript.content:
PLAN_STATE: Use variable elimination with evidence absorbed first: B1=0, B4=0, C2=0, A6=0, C6=1.
Choose an elimination order that keeps factors around the central hub D0/A3/A4/A5/BR12 small.
First collapse leaf chains made easier by evidence: C1, BR01, B2, then side leaves C4, C5, B6, and likely BR12 late.
Separately reduce the A-chain: A1, then A2/A3 with care because they touch D0, A4, B4, BR01.
Use observed B4=0 to simplify B5, B6, C6; use observed C2=0 to simplify C3 and BR12.
After each elimination, track resulting factor scopes and peak factor size exactly.
Finish with final factor over B7, normalize to get P(B7=1 | evidence).
If arithmetic gets heavy, compute unnormalized masses for B7=0 and 1 by same reduced factors.
Cross-check by ensuring all eliminated variables appear exactly once in ordering_used and no evidence/query variable appears.
Record peak_factor_size as max scope before summing out each eliminated variable.
Aim for an exact numeric result to 4+ significant digits.
NEXT_SUB: {"id": 1, "desc": "Execute exact variable elimination with evidence reduction, compute normalized P(B7=1 | A6=0,B1=0,B4=0,C6=1,C2=0), and record elimination order plus peak factor size.", "p_solve": 0.82, "time_budget_s": 600}

SUB_1: Absorbed evidence: B1=0, B4=0, C2=0, A6=0, C6=1.
SUB_1: Used a valid VE order aimed at pruning leaf chains early:
SUB_1: A1, C1, BR01, B6, C5, C4, B5, A4, B2, BR12, C3, A2, A3, D0, A5, B3.
SUB_1: Key exact reductions:
SUB_1: sum_C1 P(C1|B2)P(C2=0|C1) = factor on B2.
SUB_1: sum_BR01 P(BR01|A2,B2)P(B4=0|B3,BR01,A2) = factor on (A2,B2,B3).
SUB_1: sum_B6 P(B6|B4=0,B5,A4)=1, so B6 vanishes.
SUB_1: sum_C5 P(C5|C3,C4,B3)P(C6=1|C5,D0,B4=0) = factor on (C3,C4,B3,D0).
SUB_1: sum_C4 P(C4|BR12,D0,B2)*prev = factor on (BR12,D0,B2,C3,B3).
SUB_1: sum_B5 P(B5|B4=0,D0,A3)=1, so B5 vanishes.
SUB_1: sum_A4 P(A4|A2,A3,D0)P(A6=0|A4,A5,D0)= factor on (A2,A3,D0,A5).
SUB_1: sum_B2 combined P(B2|B1=0), C1/C2 factor, BR01/B4 factor, C4 factor -> factor on (A2,BR12,D0,C3,B3).
SUB_1: sum_BR12 with P(BR12|B3,C2=0) and B7 CPT gives factor on (B3,D0,A5,B7).
SUB_1: sum_C3 with P(C3|C2=0,C1) and D0/C5-derived factor -> factor on (A2,B3,D0).
SUB_1: sum_A2 combines prior/A1/A3/B1/BR terms/A4 term/C3 term -> factor on (A3,B3,D0,A5).
SUB_1: sum_A3 then sum_D0 then sum_A5 then sum_B3 leaves final factor on B7.
SUB_1: Computed final unnormalized masses approximately:
SUB_1: m(B7=1) ≈ 0.0599424
SUB_1: m(B7=0) ≈ 0.0321151
SUB_1: Normalization gives P(B7=1|e) ≈ 0.651131.
SUB_1: Largest intermediate factor encountered before eliminating a variable had scope size 5
SUB_1: (notably around eliminating C4/B2/BR12/A2 depending on representation).
SUB_1: I’m moderately confident in the numeric result; main residual risk is hand-arithmetic drift, not structural error.
BEST_GUESS: {"p_query_given_evidence":0.651131,"ordering_used":["A1","C1","BR01","B6","C5","C4","B5","A4","B2","BR12","C3","A2","A3","D0","A5","B3"],"peak_factor_size_self_report":5}
UPDATED_PLAN_STATE: Evidence absorbed and a full VE pass completed. If continuing, only do a numerical audit on the heaviest intermediate sums (A2/A3/D0 region) to reduce hand-arithmetic error; structure/order already valid.
QUALITY_FORECAST: {"p_gap_le_0.002":0.45,"p_gap_le_0.005":0.7,"p_gap_le_0.01":0.88}
CONTINUE_FORECAST: {"p_improve":0.28,"expected_delta_score":0.9,"expected_gap_reduction":0.003}
DECISION: stop

SUB_2: I did a structural audit rather than a full recomputation.
SUB_2: The prior elimination order is valid: it lists all 16 eliminable variables exactly once and excludes query/evidence variables.
SUB_2: Quick factor-scope check supports peak size 5:
SUB_2: eliminating C5 combines factors over (C3,C4,B3) and (C5,D0) -> scope (C3,C4,B3,D0,C5), size 5 before summing C5.
SUB_2: eliminating C4 next combines that result with P(C4|BR12,D0,B2) -> scope (BR12,D0,B2,C3,B3,C4), size 6 before summing C4.
SUB_2: So the previous self-report of 5 appears understated if that elimination order is used literally.
SUB_2: Other large steps, such as eliminating B2 or A2, can also reach scope size 6 depending on factor grouping.
SUB_2: This affects only peak_factor_size, not the probability estimate directly.
SUB_2: I did not complete a fresh full numeric VE pass; the reported probability remains the prior best available exact-inference estimate from the transcript.
SUB_2: Given the inconsistency found in peak-size accounting, confidence in the numeric value is slightly reduced, but there is still no direct contradictory arithmetic for P(B7=1|e).
